channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrats ramp up town halls in GOP districts
item.description: House Democrats are ramping up their aggressive strategy of conducting town halls in Republican-held districts.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: GOP vows to stop lone activist judges from halting Trump agenda
item.description: Republicans are bashing a federal judge's ruling blocking the deportation of suspected gang members and say they are moving to ensure such an occurrence does not happen again.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court upholds a Biden-era rule regulating ghost guns
item.description: The court says ghost guns, which are virtually untraceable weapons that require assembly, are considered "firearms" and subject to ATF regulation.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: University of Minnesota leaders say ICE has detained a graduate student
item.description: The University of Minnesota says one of its graduate students is being detained by U.S. immigration authorities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he won't 'fire people' over Signal messages, reiterates support of national security team
item.description: President Donald Trump is making his clearest commitment to not fire anyone over an embarrassing accidental leak of his administration’s plans for an airstrike against the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, have received email notices of their mass firing — the latest step in the Trump administration’s government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Richard Carlbom ascends to top role at Minnesota DFL Party as its first new chair in 14 years
item.description: Richard Carlbom was chosen as the Minnesota DFL Party chair, rising to the top post after years of working behind the scenes on party elections and causes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Critics see Trump attacks on the 'Black Smithsonian' as an effort to sanitize racism in US history
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump's order accusing the Smithsonian Institution of not reflecting American history notes correctly that the country's Founding Fathers declared that “all men are created equal.” But it doesn't mention that the founders enshrined slavery into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump appoints Sara Carter as next drug czar, says she 'will lead the charge to protect' US
item.description: President Donald Trump appointed Sara Carter as drug czar, saying she will "lead the charge to protect our nation and save our children from the scourge of drugs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump releases powerful video highlighting US-Greenland bond after deadly Nazi attack
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a powerful video Friday highlighting the strong historical bonds between Greenland and the United States amid ongoing efforts to annex the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: A Wisconsin appellate court has denied the state Democratic attorney general’s request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk says 'civilization will disappear' unless birth rates change
item.description: "The birth rate is very low in almost every country, and unless that changes, civilization will disappear," Musk said in an interview with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey's GOP primary for governor could pivot on Trump, a part-time resident
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — The most important Republican in New Jersey's race for governor this year might well be a part-time resident of Bedminster who burnished his reputation and his brand near the Atlantic City Boardwalk.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why Trump's push for frigid Greenland is about icing out US adversaries
item.description: President Donald Trump’s push to acquire Greenland highlights the island’s growing strategic value, from rare earth mineral wealth to its role in Arctic defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military review of fitness standards will find array of tests, but higher requirements for combat
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s decision to review military standards on combat and physical fitness and appearance opens a Pandora’s box of the widely different rules among the services.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kalshi app aims to democratize markets, says stocks are for elites
item.description: Kalshi says it’s the first regulated exchange where people “can buy and sell contracts on the outcome of events.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Dan Goldman supports reforms to NY's evidence laws
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York State budget deadline is this upcoming Tuesday. One of Gov. Kathy Hochul’s priorities for this session is to reform New York’s evidence laws, which were passed in 2019. The governor’s proposal includes three major provisions.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court to hear case banning Medicaid for Planned Parenthood
item.description: A Medicaid user says South Carolina blocked her right to visit Planned Parenthood for nonabortion health care.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Top Minnesota election official says Trump is attempting ‘unlawful’ power grab
item.description: Minnesota Secretary of State Steve Simon said President Donald Trump’s recent executive order targeting elections was “unlawful.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Trump: House should take up Senate-passed DC funding fix immediately
item.description: President Trump is urging swift passage of legislation to fix what members have called a “mistake” by Congress that forced significant cuts to Washington, D.C.’s local budget. Trump on Friday wrote on Truth Social that the House should “immediately” take up the “D.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DFLers at Minnesota Capitol rip Musk’s ideas while the GOP wants a closer look
item.description: Billionaire and top Trump adviser Elon Musk is reshaping the federal government. In Minnesota, Democrats are increasingly turning Musk mentions into a punching bag. And Republicans say his work could be replicated in St. Paul.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Mark Kelly hits back at Musk: ‘Elon standing with' billionaires
item.description: Sen. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) slammed what he called senior Trump adviser Elon Musk’s loyalties to billionaires Thursday after Musk accused the Arizona senator and decorated combat veteran of putting other countries ahead of the United States, the latest salvo in their rancorous feud.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Foragers want a seat at the rulemaking table as DNR mulls mushroom, berry bag limits
item.description: The DNR is considering bag limits on how much food people can take from state-managed lands. That gallon limit is ringing alarm bells for foraging groups like the Minnesota Mycological Society.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
